name: Attack from Castillo de La Mota – circular by Las Salinas and Medina del Campo
name_original: Ataquines desde Castillo de La Mota – circular por Las Salinas y Medina del Campo
description: This moderate 38.1-mile road cycling route circles Castillo de La Mota, offering scenic views and historical landmarks near Medina del Campo
region_intro: Road cycling around Medina Del Campo offers varied landscapes and well-suited terrain for outdoor activities. The region is situated in the central part of the Douro basin, characterized by the Castilian countryside, rural Valladolid, pine forests, and vineyards. Cyclists will find mostly paved surfaces, with flat sections for speed and occasional climbs that challenge endurance. The area's central plateau location provides open skies and a landscape shaped by agricultural activities. Best road cycling routes around Medina Del Campo The most popular road cycling route is Attack from Castillo de La Mota – circular by Las Salinas and Medina del Campo, a 38.1 miles (61.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 23 minutes to complete. This moderate route passes historical sites like the Castle of La Mota. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Cantalpiedra y Madrigal de las Altas Torres — circular desde Medina el Campo, a moderate 55.4 miles (89.2 km) path. This route explores the Castilian countryside and connects historical towns. Local road cyclists also love the Route of the Castles: Medina del Campo, Íscar, and Olmedo, a 51.3 miles (82.6 km) trail leading through rural Valladolid and pine forests, often completed in about 3 hours 14 minutes. Road cycling around Medina Del Campo is defined by the Castilian countryside, pine forests, and vineyards. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy to more challenging routes. The routes in Medina Del Campo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars. More than 270 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Medina Del Campo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Medina del Campo?
A: There are 29 road cycling routes available around Medina del Campo on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 6 easy, 22 moderate, and 1 difficult option.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Medina del Campo?
A: The region around Medina del Campo is characterized by the Castilian countryside, rural Valladolid, pine forests, and vineyards. Cyclists will find mostly paved surfaces, with flat sections ideal for speed and occasional climbs to challenge endurance. The area's central plateau location offers open skies and a landscape shaped by agricultural activities.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Medina del Campo?
A: Yes, Medina del Campo offers 6 easy road cycling routes, making it suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes provide a gentle introduction to the region's beautiful landscapes.

Q: What historical landmarks or points of interest can I see along road cycling routes in Medina del Campo?
A: Many routes pass by significant historical sites. For instance, the spectacular 15th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454786" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of La Mota&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent landmark often incorporated into rides. Other notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5621377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of San Antolín&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5779390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge over the Adaja River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any specific routes that pass by notable castles or historical sites?
A: Absolutely. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1153266881/attack-from-castillo-de-la-mota-circular-by-las-salinas-and-medina-del-campo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Attack from Castillo de La Mota – circular by Las Salinas and Medina del Campo&lt;/a&gt; directly passes by the iconic Castle of La Mota. Another option, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350852852/route-of-the-castles-medina-del-campo-iscar-and-olmedo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Castles: Medina del Campo, Íscar, and Olmedo&lt;/a&gt;, takes you through several historical towns and past castles in the rural Valladolid area.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Medina del Campo?
A: Medina del Campo, located in the central plateau of Spain, generally offers pleasant conditions for road cycling during spring and autumn. These seasons typically provide milder temperatures and less extreme weather, making for more comfortable rides. Summer can be quite hot, while winter might bring colder temperatures.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the Medina del Campo area?
A: Yes, many routes in the region are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350874339/cantalpiedra-y-madrigal-de-las-altas-torres-circular-desde-medina-el-campo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cantalpiedra y Madrigal de las Altas Torres — circular desde Medina el Campo&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1152369563/la-mota-castle-and-rueda-vineyards-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mota Castle and Rueda Vineyards Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offer varied distances and scenery.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Medina del Campo?
A: The road cycling routes in Medina del Campo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Over 270 road cyclists have explored the area using komoot, often praising the varied terrain, historical points of interest, and the scenic Castilian countryside.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in Medina del Campo?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, the 6 easy routes available in Medina del Campo are generally suitable for families or those with less cycling experience. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances, providing a more relaxed experience for all.

Q: Are there options for public transport that accommodate bikes in Medina del Campo?
A: Information regarding specific public transport options that accommodate bikes in Medina del Campo is not readily available. It is advisable to check with local transport providers directly for their policies on bicycle carriage if you plan to use public transport.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Medina del Campo?
A: While specific designated parking areas for cyclists are not detailed, Medina del Campo, as a town, offers various public parking facilities. It is recommended to look for parking near the starting points of your chosen routes, particularly in or around the town center, which often provides convenient access to the surrounding cycling network.

Q: Can I find routes that explore the vineyards around Medina del Campo?
A: Yes, the region is known for its extensive vineyards, particularly in the Rueda area. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1152369563/la-mota-castle-and-rueda-vineyards-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mota Castle and Rueda Vineyards Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent option for cyclists wishing to explore these scenic wine-producing landscapes.
tips: Ataquines is a small agricultural town on the northern plateau, near the border with the provinces of Ávila and Segovia. Like many rural towns in Spain, the church is the most notable monument in the city with a tall tower visible from a distance.

The Palacio de las Salinas Spa is an idyllic place to relax and enjoy a unique environment. In addition, its strategic location allows you to explore nearby places such as Tordesillas, Olmedo and Rueda. If you are looking for a refuge to take care of yourself and disconnect, this spa is an excellent option.

The medieval Palacio de las Dueñas is an interesting architectural monument with a well-preserved patio that surrounds it with a cloister and typical columns of the time. Today it is used as a high school, not like the schools I went to.

The Zapardiel River runs through the town of Medina del Campo on its way to the Duero River downstream. The river is born in the sierra de Ávila. River levels depend on the amount of rainfall, and actually the channel the river passes through is dry today.

The Castillo de la Mota, also known as Castillo de La Mota, is a medieval fortress located in the town of Medina del Campo, in the province of Valladolid, Spain. Its name comes from its location on a high hill, a mote, from where it overlooks the city and its surroundings.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 61.3 kilometers long and is estimated to take around 2 hours and 23 minutes to complete on a racebike, requiring good fitness.

faqs.question: What is the starting point for this cycling route and is parking available?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one, often starting near the historic Castle of La Mota in Medina del Campo. Parking is generally available in the vicinity of the castle or within Medina del Campo, which is well-suited for cycling tourism.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily features mostly well-paved surfaces, making it generally easy to ride for road cyclists. The 'Camino de las Salinas' section, which is part of this area, is described as a green track.

faqs.question: What historical sites or landmarks will I see on this ride?
faqs.answer: You'll pass by several notable points of interest, including the impressive Castle of La Mota, the Church of Ataquines, the Balneario Palacio de las Salinas, and the Palacio de Dueñas Courtyard, Medina del Campo. You'll also encounter the Zapardiel River in Medina del Campo.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is categorized as 'moderate' and requires good fitness. While the surfaces are mostly well-paved, it's best suited for regular cyclists rather than absolute beginners due to its length and moderate difficulty.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region is generally pleasant for cycling during spring and autumn, offering milder temperatures. Summer can be quite hot, while winter might bring colder conditions. Checking local weather forecasts before your ride is always recommended.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with sections of several official trails, including the Ruta de Carlos V, Camino de Santiago del Sureste, and Etapa 25: Medina del Campo - Sieteiglesias de Trabancos, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to cycle this public road cycling route. However, some attractions along the way, like the Castle of La Mota, may have their own entrance fees if you wish to visit them.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a road cycling route, it's generally not designed with dogs in mind, especially given the distance and potential for traffic. It's best to leave your furry friends at home for this particular ride.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect views of the historic Castillo de La Mota, open fields, and natural landscapes. The 'Camino de las Salinas' section offers glimpses of a pine forest and opportunities to observe local flora and fauna like thyme and various birds.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: Medina del Campo, being a significant town, offers various options for cafes, restaurants, and pubs. While the route itself passes through open areas, you'll find amenities in the towns and villages it connects, particularly at the start and end points.
